title: Relabel volumes after creation
body:
### Feature request description

I want to add/modify a label after a volume has been created.

### Suggest potential solution

podman volume create test-volume
podman volume label --add foo=bar test-volume
podman volume label --replace foo=baz test-volume

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Maintaining a separate file of metadata for my volumes. Or ConditionPathExists in my podman volume restore service unit, to ensureit only runs once.

### Additional context

This is for backup and restore purposes. I am running Fedora CoreOS as container host with quadlets and podman volumes. When a new VM first starts I restore all volumes with a systemd unit that the quadlet depends on. In order for this systemd unit to only restore the volume once I want to add a label that says backup=restored.

comments.body:
Most of our architecture is designed to be immutable and not edit things, yes there are ways to modify it but I rather not go that route.
Now volumes is one thing the next person asks for containers, pods, images, etc... As such I don't think this would be a good fit for us ass it will add a fair amount of complexity.

If you only want to do things on first boot you could use the system condition ConditionFirstBoot, https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/latest/systemd.unit.html#ConditionFirstBoot=
If we wanted to do a `podman volume update` that did labels as well, that could be reasonable, given that command already exists for containers, but this would still be a large amount of work.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
